title: soap_rel_buf
text: soap_rel_buf Releases the buffer used for the amended SOAP envelope buffer or clears the context area, if dynamic memory allocation is used. #include <wsciapi.h> soap_rel_buf(int which, void *buf, void *context) Copy Parameters int which —flag that identifies the buffer to be released. An enum provided in the header file defines the available values: WSCIAPI_OUTBUF —actual value ‘0’, the buffer used for the amended SOAP envelope WSCIAPI_CONTEXT —actual value ‘1’, the context area void *buf —pointer to the buffer that is to be deallocated. This will be equal to the pointer returned in *out by either the soap_pre_request() or soap_post_request() function if the which parameter has the value WSCIAPI_OUTBUF , or it will be equal to the pointer returned in *context if the which parameter has the value WSCIAPI_CONTEXT . void *context —pointer to the context area that the implementation previously returned through either the soap_pre_request() or soap_post_request() function. Take care not to access memory through this pointer when the memory has been freed by using the buf parameter. Both pointers point to the same memory when the which parameter contains WSCIAPI_CONTEXT . Return Values None Description The function soap_rel_buf() is intended to provide flexibility in terms of memory allocation and deallocation. An implementation can allocate a block of memory, the context, which will be returned on every subsequent call of the API functions. This can be useful for the implementation to store data between calls or to implement efficient memory management for the amended SOAP envelopes. This function should be provided if dynamic memory allocation is used, either for the amended SOAP envelope buffer or for the context area. It is the implementation’s responsibility to free all allocated memory. Uniface calls this function whenever: Uniface no longer needs the amended SOAP envelope, that is, after a call to soap_pre_request() or soap_post_request() and before the next call to either function. Uniface exits, if a context area was allocated this function Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
